---
title: "Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos"
---

This website presents the MLOps version of an NLP project on French electoral manifestos, including the dataset, method, architecture, API, and baseline results.

## Project overview

This project studies whether French electoral manifestos are shaped more strongly by **party ideology** or by **candidates' socio-professional characteristics**.

Using manifesto texts from the **Archelec archive**, the project applies **topic modeling with Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA)** to identify recurring themes in political discourse across parties, professions, and election years.

## From research project to MLOps system

The initial work began as an NLP analysis project. It was then transformed into a reproducible and deployable machine learning system.

The pipeline covers the full workflow:

`data -> preprocessing -> feature preparation -> modeling -> outputs -> inference -> API`

## Why this project matters

Political manifestos are useful documents for studying how parties and candidates frame public issues.

This project makes that analysis:

- reproducible from the command line
- modular and testable
- reusable through saved artifacts
- accessible through an API
- deployable in a cloud environment

## Main research question

Are manifesto themes explained more by:

- political party affiliation?
- or socio-professional background?

## What this website contains

This documentation website presents:

- the dataset used in the pipeline
- the modeling approach
- the deployed API
- the system architecture
- the main experiment results

## Intended users

This project is designed for:

- data science students
- political science researchers
- instructors

## Project links

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/sarakpyny/MLOps-nlp-manifesto)
- [Live API documentation](https://manifesto-api-sny.lab.sspcloud.fr/docs)

## Summary

This project shows how an academic NLP workflow can be refactored into a complete MLOps system with reproducibility, modularity, testing, deployment, and public-facing documentation.
